plain_definition: The EU's banking regulator, and the lead EU-level body for MiCA's stablecoin provisions -- asset-referenced tokens (Title III) and e-money tokens (Title IV) -- as well as the crypto travel-rule guidelines implementing the recast Transfer of Funds Regulation, and technical standards on banks' own prudential treatment of crypto-asset exposures under the Capital Requirements Regulation. EBA takes over direct supervision of an ART or EMT classified 'significant', and shares supervision of a significant EMT with the issuer's home national competent authority. EBA does not itself authorise or supervise ordinary (non-significant) stablecoin issuers -- that remains an NCA function. It plays a broadly similar EU-level standard-setting role for banking and stablecoin matters to how the HKMA regulates stablecoin issuers in Hong Kong, but EBA sits above, rather than in place of, the 27 national regulators.
formal_definition: The European Banking Authority, established under Regulation (EU) No 1093/2010 (the EBA Regulation), is the EU-level authority for MiCA Titles III and IV technical standards and guidelines, for the significance classification and direct/dual supervision of significant ARTs and EMTs, and for the EBA Guidelines on information requirements under Regulation (EU) 2023/1113 (the crypto travel rule).
